detaileddescription.para: Fonts included in libharu please note that using default font does not allow UTF-8 encoding

briefdescription.para: set height to keep same scale as width scale

briefdescription.para: Parameters of document style.

Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_PdfParameters_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_PdfParameters_DestroyArray(). Use MR_PdfParameters_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_PdfParameters_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

detaileddescription.para: Constructs MR::PdfParameters elementwise. The reference to the parameter defaultFont might be preserved in the constructed object. The reference to the parameter defaultFontBold might be preserved in the constructed object. The reference to the parameter tableFont might be preserved in the constructed object. The reference to the parameter tableFontBold might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_PdfParameters_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it had previously.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::PdfParameters::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it had previously.

Ctor. Create a document, but not a page. To create a new page use newPage() method Generated from constructor MR::Pdf::Pdf. Parameter params is a single object. Parameter params has a default argument: MR::PdfParameters(), pass a null pointer to use it. The reference to the parameter params might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Pdf_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_Pdf_DestroyArray(). Use MR_Pdf_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_Pdf_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

memberdef.argsstring: (MR_Pdf *_this, const char *text, const char *text_end, const bool *isTitle)
memberdef.name: MR_Pdf_addText_bool

detaileddescription.para: Add text block in current cursor position. Move cursor. Box horizontal size is page width without offset. Box vertical size is automatically for text. horAlignment = left if isTitle - horAlignment = center, use titleFontSize Generated from method MR::Pdf::addText. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter text can not be null. If text_end is null, then text is assumed to be null-terminated. Parameter isTitle has a default argument: false, pass a null pointer to use it.

memberdef.argsstring: (MR_Pdf *_this, const char *text, const char *text_end, const MR_Pdf_TextParams *params)
memberdef.name: MR_Pdf_addText_MR_Pdf_TextParams

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::Pdf::addText. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter text can not be null. If text_end is null, then text is assumed to be null-terminated. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object.

memberdef.name: MR_Pdf_getTextWidth

detaileddescription.para: return text width Generated from method MR::Pdf::getTextWidth. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter text can not be null. If text_end is null, then text is assumed to be null-terminated. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object.

Add set of pair string - value in current cursor position. Move cursor. Box horizontal size is page width without offset. Box vertical size is automatically for text. memberdef.name: MR_Pdf_addImageFromFile

briefdescription.para: Add image from file in current cursor position. If image bigger than page size, autoscale image to page size. Move cursor.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::Pdf::addImageFromFile. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter imagePath is a UTF-8 encoded filesystem path. Parameter params can not be null. It is a single object.

memberdef.argsstring: (MR_Color color, const char *rangeMin, const char *rangeMin_end, const char *rangeMax, const char *rangeMax_end, const char *percent, const char *percent_end)
memberdef.name: MR_Pdf_PaletteRowStats_ConstructFrom

detaileddescription.para: Constructs MR::Pdf::PaletteRowStats elementwise. The reference to the parameter color might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter rangeMin can not be null. If rangeMin_end is null, then rangeMin is assumed to be null-terminated. The reference to the parameter rangeMin might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter rangeMax can not be null. If rangeMax_end is null, then rangeMax is assumed to be null-terminated. The reference to the parameter rangeMax might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter percent can not be null. If percent_end is null, then percent is assumed to be null-terminated. The reference to the parameter percent might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. memberdef.name: MR_Pdf_CellCustomParams_ConstructFrom

detaileddescription.para: Constructs MR::Pdf::CellCustomParams elementwise. Parameter colorText is optional. To keep it empty, pass a null pointer. The reference to the parameter colorText might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter colorCellBg is optional. To keep it empty, pass a null pointer. The reference to the parameter colorCellBg might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter colorCellBorder is optional. To keep it empty, pass a null pointer. The reference to the parameter colorCellBorder might be preserved in the constructed object. If text_end is null, then text is assumed to be null-terminated. Non-null text_end requires a non-null text. Parameter text is optional. To keep it empty, pass a null pointer to both it and text_end. The reference to the parameter text might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Pdf_CellCustomParams_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it had previously.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.
